sources.supports: The directory lists Nimaz Aesthetic at Maros utca 30 with phone +36 20 803 7310.
The 2026 laureate index includes Nimaz Aesthetic with a score shown by the directory.

prospects.owned_domain: No owned domain was located in the checked sources; this is not proof that none exists.
prospects.conversion_route: Public phone route is listed; a current booking flow was not verified.
prospects.contact_route: Public phone +36 20 803 7310 and directory listing; no outreach was attempted.
observations: Two current public directories support the business name, Budapest location, and continuing visibility.
The checked sources did not establish an owned domain, booking route, or responsible decision-maker.
prospects.recommended_angle: Keep the aesthetic patient-funnel and local/AI visibility hypothesis, but hold all outreach until the owner and booking route are verified.
prospects.evidence_notes: The tracker phone and address are supported. The user-supplied Google rating and review count were not independently reverified in this pass.

sources.supports: The English owned site identifies the company, services, contact details, references, and a detailed quote-request funnel. Its values section visibly repeats the label 'Responsible Employment'.
The English contact experience places a general website-feedback route alongside the detailed quote request, whose copy promises a preliminary quote within 24 hours.
The Hungarian owned site mirrors the company identity and quote funnel.
The business listing identifies Pataki Csaba as owner and managing director.
The public LinkedIn result describes Csaba Pataki as owner and managing director of the company.

prospects.conversion_route: Detailed on-page quote request plus phone +36 20 331 7451 and info@office-cleaning.hu.
prospects.decision_maker: Pataki Csaba, owner and managing director.
prospects.contact_route: Owned-site quote form, phone, public company email, and public LinkedIn profile; no outreach was attempted.
observations: The tracker website field is stale: the company has established English and Hungarian owned sites.
The owned-site funnel collects service scope, office size, frequency, timing, carpet coverage, and target start date before promising a preliminary quote.
The English contact experience presents a general website-feedback route before the stronger buyer-facing quote action.
The English homepage visibly duplicates the 'Responsible Employment' label in a trust-focused values section.
prospects.recommended_angle: Do not pitch a new website. Lead with a focused English form-hierarchy and content-QA review that protects the mature quote funnel.
prospects.evidence_notes: The company presents substantial references and a mature conversion path, so a generic infrastructure pitch would be weak. Recheck the two visible English-page observations immediately before any approved send.
prospects.commercial_gap: The English enquiry journey gives a general website-feedback route prominence alongside the detailed quote request, while a duplicated trust label remains visible on the homepage. This creates a narrow conversion and content-QA opportunity for international office managers without implying that the overall site or funnel is weak.
prospects.proof_to_use: No proof in the first touch; hold the anonymized Central European B2B services proof for a relevant follow-up.
prospects.draft_subject: One competing form on your English contact page
prospects.draft_body:
Hi Csaba,

I was reviewing Professional Office Cleaning Hungary's English enquiry journey and noticed a small conversion issue on the contact experience.

A general website-feedback route competes with the detailed quote request, even though the quote form is the stronger buyer action and promises a preliminary response within 24 hours. For an international office manager, those competing routes can make the next step less immediate. I also noticed a duplicated trust label on the English homepage.

I noted three focused form-hierarchy and English content-QA fixes. Happy to send the short review if useful.

Best,
Emmanuel Ehigbai
BridgeWorks
prospects.next_internal_action: Await Emmanuel's explicit mobile approval of the exact email destination, subject, and body.
prospects.risk_notes: Recheck the page hierarchy, duplicate label, and public email immediately before send. Do not imply the overall funnel is weak or pitch a rebuild.

prospects.decision_maker: Angyal Szilvia is listed as managing director.
prospects.contact_route: Public directory routes exist; no outreach was attempted.
observations: A current business-information source marks the company as in liquidation.
Public records conflict on the company domain, which creates an additional identity and routing risk.
prospects.recommended_angle: Reject from active acquisition. Do not spend research or outreach time unless the legal status changes and the operating identity is reverified.
prospects.evidence_notes: The liquidation signal is sufficient to remove this record from the active prospect lane.

sources.supports: The owned site provides segmented contact and quote forms, Budapest phone/email, and a broad service catalogue.

prospects.conversion_route: Contact and quote-request forms, office.budapest@firstfacility.net, and +36 1 312 4051.
prospects.decision_maker: The imprint names Radivoje Nijemcevic as CEO/owner, Gergely Szabo as CEO, and Tamas Monoki as CFO.
prospects.contact_route: Owned-site contact and quote forms plus public office email and phone; no outreach was attempted.
observations: The site has a mature multilingual service and contact architecture covering property, facility, and consulting work.
Current contact-page copy contains visible quality-control issues including 'Firts Facility Policy' and 'Projektmanagent'.
prospects.recommended_angle: Hold as a sector benchmark. If promoted later, use a narrow multilingual credibility and AI-visibility quality audit rather than a website rebuild pitch.
prospects.evidence_notes: This is a larger international group, so likely sales effort and approval cycles are higher than the first-wave SME lane.

sources.supports: The owned site presents integrated services, client testimonials, portfolio scale, and an English experience.
The contact page provides intent and service segmentation plus named senior leaders and direct routes.

prospects.conversion_route: Segmented enquiry form with general enquiry, callback, meeting, and quote intents plus named direct contacts.
prospects.decision_maker: Gabor Nagy, managing director; the site also names service-area directors.
prospects.contact_route: Owned-site enquiry form, general office route, and named senior contacts; no outreach was attempted.
observations: The site already carries extensive proof, named experts, service segmentation, and large portfolio signals.
The enquiry path lets visitors choose both intent and service area before contact, reducing routing ambiguity.
prospects.recommended_angle: Hold as a high-scale benchmark. Consider only a premium AI-search visibility or multilingual authority audit based on a specific live gap.
prospects.evidence_notes: No sufficiently sharp commercial gap was established for first-wave outreach.
